timeline.name: Tammy Miller
timeline.notes: Burgum resigned to join Trump administration; Miller did not succeed as Gov. New governor (Armstrong) elected 2024.

timeline.name: Brent Sanford
timeline.notes: Served under Gov. Doug Burgum. Did not seek another term when Burgum won re-election with Tammy Miller.

timeline.name: Drew Wrigley
timeline.notes: Appointed by Gov. Dalrymple to fill vacancy, then elected 2012.

timeline.name: Jack Dalrymple
timeline.notes: Succeeded Gov. John Hoeven who resigned to enter U.S. Senate.

timeline.name: Rosemarie Myrdal
timeline.notes: First woman elected Lt. Gov. of North Dakota.

timeline.name: Lloyd Omdahl
timeline.notes: Democratic-NPL. Appointed to fill vacancy after Meiers' death, then elected 1988.

timeline.name: Ruth Meiers
timeline.notes: Democratic-NPL. Died in office on March 19, 1987. First woman Lt. Gov. of North Dakota.

timeline.name: Wayne Sanstead
timeline.notes: Democratic-NPL. Served two terms under Gov. Arthur Link.

timeline.name: Richard F. Larsen
timeline.notes: Served under Democratic Gov. William Guy (split ticket).

timeline.name: Frank A. Wenstrom
timeline.notes: Served under Democratic Gov. William Guy (split ticket). Note: ND had 2-year terms until 1964.

timeline.name: Orville W. Hagen
timeline.notes: Served under Democratic Gov. William Guy (split ticket).

timeline.name: Clarence P. Dahl
timeline.notes: Third non-consecutive term as Lt. Gov.
